repo_truth: Fresh read-only Review 6 of the exact fifteen-file HCM-1.3 planning subject. Reviews 1, 2, and 4 produced bounded findings that were remediated; Reviews 3 and 5 failed before verdict because the selected model was at capacity. Authoritative packet bytes are unchanged since the exact ledger allowlist remediation. The complete subject manifests all dispatch, remediation, and capacity-stop evidence. Independently derive a complete verdict from live authority/source; no prior verdict is inherited.
allowed_scope: Remain read-only and replay the sorted fifteen-entry manifest and aggregate fingerprint before review.
Review every byte of the phase map, current SPEC/plan/unchecked todo, all proof/evidence files, and all five predecessor review dispatches.
Read unchanged control-pack authority, HCM-1.1/HCM-1.2 packets/closeouts, live HCM-1.2 source/tests/definitions/package proof/public APIs, and official dependency documentation only as needed.
Run read-only Markdown, link, Git, handoff/schema, file-allowlist, fixed-surface/facade, API feasibility, TDD ordering, package-topology, and security checks.
Return findings first with exact file/line evidence through the built-in channel.
non_goals: No edits, staging, commits, new dispatches, global handoffs, ledger writes, or user task hops.
No Rust, Cargo, runtime/product, setup/doctor, canonical YAML/content, HCM-1.4+, SDK, CLI, Tauri, Substrate, adapter, contract, or dock implementation.
Do not infer an unspecified schema, ID, fingerprint, default, compatibility, migration, fallback, product adoption, or later-slice behavior.
Do not treat invalidated subjects or either capacity stop as approval.
tasks: Invoke required skills in order, remain read-only, replay the exact fifteen-entry manifest/aggregate, and return BLOCKED if any byte differs.

Review findings first in Critical, Required, Optional, Nit order. Every actionable finding must include severity, exact file/line, violated authority/invariant/gate, concrete failure, smallest valid remediation, and invalidated proof.
Verify exact existing/new file allowlists against every implementation, review, proof, first-commit, and second-commit task, including the sole permitted deterministic ledger.jsonl rebuild.
Reproduce the literal 29-file fixed-surface/facade set, inspect re-export facades independently, and verify every later-owner disposition.

Re-verify every substantive remediation: exact public value APIs/borrowing, genuine red-before-production task order, complete fixed-surface/facade inventory, exact closeout allowlist, and truthful evidence lineage.

Verify the registry owner boundary, unchanged HCM-1.2 APIs, roles/capabilities/custom IDs, data-driven fields, fail-fast/provider/order/structural validation, and no enum/legacy/fallback/dynamic/product-adoption escape.
Verify trusted path/source/identity/fingerprint/duplicate/compatibility/dependency/error protections and no unjustified limits or executable semantic hooks.
Verify TDD positives, N-N+1, negative/security/regression/Windows/package/scope proof, classification ceiling, exit gate, stop conditions, and implementation separability.
Verify literal package member set equality plus per-member hash/size remains required and no count-only proof or fixture becomes package authority.
Verify all subject files agree on planning-only status, non-goals, fresh review, two-commit closeout, future implementation selector boundary, and terminal stop before HCM-1.4.
Verify todo is unchecked, planning is docs-only, all coupled bytes are manifested, and historical invalidated/capacity evidence is non-authoritative.
Return CLEAN only if there is no valid Critical or Required finding; otherwise return CHANGES_REQUIRED or BLOCKED. Make no edits.
stop_conditions: Return BLOCKED if the manifest/aggregate, required authority, live source, or mandatory read-only verification cannot be reproduced.
Return CHANGES_REQUIRED rather than filling a missing schema, identifier, fingerprint, default, compatibility, migration, fallback, product-adoption, or later-slice decision by inference.
Return to the parent if remediation would require Rust/runtime work, broader control-pack authority, HCM-1.4+, or a global handoff/ledger mutation.
